title: Penducella dactyla

However, if you are referring to the plant "Plectranthus amboinicus" (also known as Coleus amboinicus or Mexican mint or Penducella dactyla) then I can provide the following information:

Plectranthus amboinicus is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa, known for its edible leaves and stems, and its potential health benefits.

The health benefits of Plectranthus amboinicus include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to prevent infections, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

The bioactive constituents of Plectranthus amboinicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Plectranthus amboinicus include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, including digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Penducella dactyla is classified as a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is further grouped into the subfamily Asteroideae and the tribe Anthemideae.
